name: Fritz Reiner
abstract: Frederick Martin Reiner (Hungarian: Reiner Frigyes; December 19, 1888 – November 15, 1963) was an American conductor of opera and symphonic music in the twentieth century. Hungarian born and trained, he emigrated to the United States in 1922, where he rose to prominence as a conductor with several orchestras.

about_quotes.speaker: William Russell
about_quotes.evidence: Which reminds me once of when we interviewed... Muggsy Spanier? ... And he was telling about one time Fritz Reiner, who was probably the most critical conductor...
